rationale: Startup env seeding now refreshes the canonical GITHUB_TOKEN managed secret from the preferred GitHub env token so stale alias switches cannot leave runtime auth on an outdated stored token.
Top-level Codex review summary only; the actionable inline finding was handled directly.
Startup env seeding now imports every nonblank GitHub token alias present instead of stopping at the first one, and tests cover multi-alias import behavior.
Top-level Gemini review summary only; the underlying inline suggestion was addressed in code and tests.
Added startup secret env-seeding coverage for GH_TOKEN and GITHUB_PAT, including canonical GITHUB_TOKEN refresh semantics.
Whitespace-only env values are now normalized away before import so they cannot create active managed secrets or suppress the dashboard warning.
Copilot PR overview only; it did not introduce an additional actionable request beyond the inline comments already classified.
Removed the startup log detail derived from the managed-secret candidate map so the sync path no longer emits secret-adjacent data that GitHub Advanced Security flags as clear-text logging.
Changed the checklist's feature link from an absolute local path to the repo-relative ../spec.md target.
Unchecked the technology-agnostic checklist assertions and clarified the notes so the checklist now matches the spec's implementation-detail content.
Informational review summary only; its actionable feedback is already represented by the inline review comments above.
Positive review with no requested change.

Replaced render-time lazy construction with a static module-level registry of lazy page components.
Changed supported-page detection to Object.hasOwn and added a regression test for inherited keys such as toString.
Updated the 503 UI-assets message to reference the shared mission-control entrypoint and the page being rendered, and covered it in tests.
Removed the unused TemplateResponse data_wide_panel context so layout state comes only from boot_payload.initialData.layout.dataWidePanel.

Updated parseCommandBehavior to throw on invalid JSON or non-object input instead of silently returning null.
Updated test_profile_selector_provider_id_routes_to_openrouter to use execution_profile_ref='auto' with profile_selector={provider_id: 'openrouter'} instead of explicit profile ref.
Updated test_profile_selector_falls_back_to_lower_priority_when_high_disabled to use profile_selector-based routing instead of explicit low-priority ref.

Added missing await to WorkflowEnvironment.start_time_skipping() in all 5 new tests to match existing test convention.

This test verifies cooldown attachment at the manager workflow level. Exercising the full 429→cooldown-reporting path from the adapter would require adding a mock adapter that returns 429, which is out of scope for this PR's focus on manager-level cooldown tracking. The current approach correctly verifies that the manager stores cooldown against the right profile.
Same as 3034774067 — routing test now uses profile_selector with execution_profile_ref='auto'.
Same as 3034774070 — fallback test now uses profile_selector with execution_profile_ref='auto'.
Changed to 'import type { ProviderProfile }' to avoid runtime import of a type-only symbol.
Added unit tests for parseCommandBehavior, parseTags, parsePriority, and parseClearEnvKeys in ProviderProfilesManager.test.tsx.
Copilot pull request overview summary, no action needed.
Restricted single-put completion retries to visibility errors only and added a regression test for non-visibility storage failures.
Restructured the frontend completion retries to use an explicit initial-attempt plus retry-schedule loop with valid indexes on every iteration.
Frontend completion retries now require a 409 response with structured `detail.code` and message instead of matching on plain error text alone.
Backend retry tests now monkeypatch the single-put retry delays to zero so the unit suite stays fast and deterministic.
The UI retry test now overrides the retry delay schedule to near-zero values instead of waiting for wall-clock backoff intervals.
This was an informational PR overview summary, not an actionable review request.
The backend now logs `retry pending` only when another retry is actually scheduled.
